mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls signal expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS increases month-over-month, or if the Rank Information Coefficient falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment exceeds 8%, equity markets have priced severe recession risk; subsequent increases in industrial production signal an economic rebound that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 8% and INDPRO rises month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: The joint combination of rising retail sales and falling unemployment confirms that consumption growth is employment-funded rather than credit-driven; this sustainable demand expansion drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal of rising RSAFS and falling UNRATE fails to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or if the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a zero interest rate policy environment, positive momentum in industrial production signals marginal revenue expansion for corporations; because discount rates are floored, this production growth translates directly into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive momentum in INDPRO does not produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or if Rank IC < 0.03 in that regime.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 500 thousand units signal durable goods and construction sector earnings expansion; this threshold indicates sufficient residential investment activity to drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of weeks when HOUST exceeds 500 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high unemployment above 7% suppresses wage growth and consumer spending; increases in the unemployment rate predict negative S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations are revised downward over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE exceeds 7% and rises month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation accelerating while the federal funds rate is below 0.25% generates sharply negative real interest rates; this financial repression forces capital out of cash and into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of accelerating CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS below 0.25% does not produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth surprises reveal aggregate demand strength that supports corporate revenue expansion; higher-than-prior-quarter GDP readings predict positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if S&P 500 60-day forward returns following above-median quarterly GDP growth are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or if Rank IC < 0.02.
